AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH-SOUTHEAST OF NEWPORT AND TRAVELED APPROXIMATELY 4.8 MILES BEFORE LIFTING JUST EAST OF THE JUNIATA RIVER. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 300 YARDS, AND MAXIMUM WINDS WERE AROUND 105 MPH. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE DOWNED BY THE TORNADO, AND ONE TREE FELL ON A PICKUP TRUCK.

*** 1 FATAL, 2 INJ *** THREE KAYAKERS ON THE WEST BRANCH OF THE SUSQUEHANNA RIVER PULLED ASHORE WHEN THE WEATHER WORSENED. ON SHORE, A TREE FELL ON THEM. TWO OF THE KAYAKERS WERE TRANSPORTED TO THE HOSPITAL WITH UNKNOWN INJURIES. THE THIRD PERSON WAS A FATALITY.
